DomHand lab: dropdowns, grids, Yes/No groups, shadow DOM

Interactions append to the log at the bottom. Inject __ff helpers before testing shadow-piercing extraction.

1. Native radios — prior employment (fieldset / legend)

Typical ATS wording; match desired_value Yes or No (avoid substring-only heuristics on labels).

Have you previously worked for ACME Corporation in a full-time role?

2. ARIA radiogroup — Yes / No buttons

Have you ever been employed by Goldman Sachs?

3. Native <select>

4. ARIA combobox + listbox (opens on button)

  • Apple
  • Banana
  • Cherry

5. Searchable dropdown (input + filtered options)

  • Los Angeles
  • San Francisco
  • New York
  • Austin

6. Async search — list shows "No options" until you type (then real rows)

Mimics async menus: open/focus shows a placeholder row; typing filters the backing list into role=option rows. DomHand should open/focus, type a search term, wait, then match/click.

    7. role="grid" + role="gridcell" (country picker simulation)

    United States
    Canada
    United Kingdom
    Germany
    Japan
    Australia

    Mirrors Fusion-style list rows; DomHand discovery includes gridcell options.

    8. Input + HTML datalist

    9. Shadow DOM (nested controls)

    10. Composite phone — country code LOV + national tel

    Selected country is visible on a readonly role="textbox" inside role="combobox" (inner input.value may be empty). National digits live in a separate type="tel" control — matches split widgets that confuse fill/assess when only the inner input is read.

    Phone Number *
    United States +1
    • United States +1
    • Canada +1
    • United Kingdom +44

    11. Visa sponsorship dependency — parent pill reveals visa type combobox

    Harsh regression target: selecting the parent sponsorship pill reveals a required visa type combobox. The field only counts as complete after the dropdown option is clicked and the commit marker updates.

    Will you now or in the future require visa sponsorship? *
    • H-1B
    • F-1 OPT
    • TN
    • N/A
    Committed value:

    12. Workday-style skills selectinput (prompt search required)

    Harsh regression target: clicking the input alone shows No Items.; a trusted click on promptSearchButton is required before search results can commit a chip.

    0 items selected

      13. Workday-style language proficiency repeater

      Mirrors the repeated language row where semantic profile values like Native / bilingual must map to live options like 5 - Fluent for Comprehension and Overall.

      Language 2 = English
